retry_reason: Failure only occurs on memory allocation for the new node or hardif_neigh. Immediate retry is unlikely to succeed because available memory is unlikely to change between calls. Caller should propagate the NULL return and handle the error.
contract_summary: This function returns a referenced batadv_neigh_node (reference count already incremented) to the caller. It first attempts to find an existing node via batadv_neigh_node_get; if found, it takes a reference on that node and returns it. If not found, it creates a new batadv_neigh_node, initializes its fields, takes an internal reference, and also acquires a reference from the hardif_neigh node. The caller receives the node with a fresh reference that must be released via batadv_neigh_node_put. The function acquires and releases the orig_node->neigh_list_lock internally. On failure (memory allocation error), it returns NULL.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to get-or-create a batadv_neigh_node and return it with an acquired reference. Includes necessary initialization (address copy, timestamp, list init) but these are minimal and intrinsic to the creation lifecycle. The hardif_neigh get-or-create is also refcount management. The overall function is a classic get-or-create wrapper.

check.reason: All four operations are consistent with the code. (1) The 'get' on return (index 0) corresponds to the explicit kref_get in the new-node path. (2) The 'set' on return corresponds to kref_init from the new-node path. (3) The second 'get' on return corresponds to the implicit get from batadv_neigh_node_get when an existing node is found. (4) The 'get' on parameter 2 (hard_iface) corresponds to kref_get on &hard_iface->refcount. All access paths and type chains match the callee info and the actual struct definitions, and segment counts are equal in each case.
